nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: Emma Malebisse was born in 1184, in Acaster Malbis, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married Robert Meynell in 1204, in Acaster Malbis, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in Cleveland, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Emma Malebisseは1184年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアアカスター マルビスで生まれた。1204年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアアカスター マルビスでRobert Meynellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアクレベランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma Malebisse(은)는 1184년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 악아스터 맬비스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1204년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 악아스터 맬비스에서 Robert Meynell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 클레벨랜드에서 사망했다.
